title: Floating Table with a Door Top
summary: A hollow-core interior door becomes a table top, resting on a frame built to leave a shadow gap under every edge so the top reads as floating.
description:
The trick here is entirely in the gap: the leg-and-apron frame is built to sit inset 1/2" from the top edge on every side, so once the door is set on top, there's a visible reveal all the way around that makes the tabletop look like it's floating above the base rather than sitting on it. A flush hollow-core door — no bore holes, no hinge mortises — is what makes this affordable at table-top size; a comparable solid slab of that size in real hardwood would cost many times more.

Build the frame first and get every leg-to-apron gap consistent before the door goes on — once the top is down, an uneven reveal on one side is very visible and there's no adjusting it without pulling the top back off.

tools.note: Keeping every leg assembly square is what makes the floating gap read evenly.

materials.name: Flush hollow-core interior door, 32" x 80", no bores or hinges

steps.title: Build the table frame
steps.body: Construct the table frame from 1x3s using 2" screws and glue, screwing through the inner aprons into the under-table supports. Space the supports about 12" apart along the length.

steps.title: Attach the end legs
steps.body: Attach the end legs with 1-1/4" screws and glue, leaving a 1/2" gap at the top — this is the reveal that makes the top look like it floats. Check every leg for square as you go.

steps.title: Attach the side legs
steps.body: Attach the side-of-leg pieces to the table frame with 1-1/4" screws and glue, then attach them to the end legs down the sides, keeping the outside edges flush.

steps.title: Attach the aprons
steps.body: Keep the top edge of the side and end aprons flush with the legs, leaving that same 1/2" gap from the top of the frame. Fasten with 1-1/4" nails and glue — screws work too, but nails are quicker and hide more easily since this joint isn't carrying significant load.

steps.title: Sand, finish, and set the door on top
steps.body: Sand and finish the entire frame before setting the door on top — it's much easier to finish the base without the top in the way. Once finished, set the door top onto the frame, checking the reveal gap is even on all four sides.
materials: Flush hollow-core interior door, 32" x 80", no bores or hinges

images.alt: Modern table built from a flush hollow-core door with a floating gap under the top
